In LTL, this can be represented by stating that whenever prop_1 happens, prop_0 must have happened before (i.e., it is not possible for prop_1 to occur before prop_0). This is commonly captured as: 'It is always the case that if prop_1 occurs, then previously (historically) prop_0 has occurred.'
